release_notes: HOTFIX — orphan reconcile now targets only the Chrome browser (parent) process, not its renderer/gpu/utility children. 0.3.92's WMI query matched any chrome.exe whose CommandLine contained the user-data-dir, but every child process is spawned with --user-data-dir too. WM_CLOSE on a renderer is a no-op (no top-level window), yet leaves the renderer marked-to-exit; the browser then waits on coordinated children that aren't responding, hits the 8s graceful timeout, gets force-killed, and the user-data-dir ends up in a dirty state. Next spawn opens chrome.exe but no window appears. Fixed by excluding processes with --type= in their command line — only the browser parent matches, and Chrome itself shuts its children down cleanly when its top-level window receives WM_CLOSE.
